content.question: 1. I don't know how I can make it up to you for spoiling your plans.
content.answer: I don't know how I can make amends for spoiling your plans. || I do not know how I can make amends for spoiling your plans. / I don't know how I can make amends for spoiling your plans / I do not know how I can make amends for spoiling your plans

content.question: 2. I don't mind staying in on a Saturday night if I have good company.
content.answer: I'm not averse to staying in on a Saturday night if I have good company. || I am not averse to staying in on a Saturday night if I have good company. / I'm not averse to staying in on a Saturday night if I have good company / I am not averse to staying in on a Saturday night if I have good company

content.question: 3. The suspect could not explain why he had sand on his boots.
content.answer: The suspect could not account for the sand on his boots. || The suspect couldn't account for the sand on his boots. / The suspect could not account for the fact he had sand on his boots. / The suspect couldn't account for the fact he had sand on his boots. / The suspect could not account for having sand on his boots. / The suspect couldn't account for having sand on his boots. / The suspect could not account for the sand on his boots / The suspect couldn't account for the sand on his boots / The suspect could not account for the fact he had sand on his boots / The suspect couldn't account for the fact he had sand on his boots / The suspect could not account for having sand on his boots / The suspect couldn't account for having sand on his boots

content.question: 4. Miss Hayes will explain the day-to-day running of the office to you.
content.answer: Miss Hayes will acquaint you with the day-to-day running of the office. || Miss Hayes'll acquaint you with the day-to-day running of the office. / Miss Hayes will acquaint you with the day-to-day running of the office / Miss Hayes'll acquaint you with the day-to-day running of the office / Miss Hayes will acquaint you with the day to day running of the office. / Miss Hayes will acquaint you with the day to day running of the office

content.question: 5. What he told me made me very curious to hear the rest of the story.
content.answer: What he told me whetted my appetite for the rest of the story. || What he told me whetted my appetite for the rest of the story

content.question: 6. The burglar was caught while stealing the jewels.
content.answer: The burglar was caught in the act of stealing the jewels. || The burglar was caught in the act of stealing the jewels

content.question: 7. My father doesn't like people to smoke without asking first.
content.answer: My father doesn't approve of people smoking without asking first. || My father does not approve of people smoking without asking first. / My father doesn't approve of people smoking without asking first / My father does not approve of people smoking without asking first

content.question: 8. Those official files cannot be seen by the public until the 21st century.
content.answer: The public will not have access to those official files until the 21st century. || The public won't have access to those official files until the 21st century. / The public will not have access to those official files until the twenty-first century. / The public won't have access to those official files until the twenty-first century. / The public will not have access to those official files until the 21st century / The public won't have access to those official files until the 21st century / The public will not have access to those official files until the twenty-first century / The public won't have access to those official files until the twenty-first century

content.question: 9. She is very precious to him.
content.answer: She is the apple of his eye. || She's the apple of his eye. / She is the apple of his eye / She's the apple of his eye

content.question: 10. You should consider the fact that he hasn't spoken French for years.
content.answer: You should make allowances for the fact that he hasn't spoken French for years. || You should make allowances for the fact that he has not spoken French for years. / You should make allowances for his not speaking French for years. / You should make allowances for having spoken French for years. / You should make allowances for his having spoken French for years. / You should make allowances for the fact that he hasn't spoken French for years / You should make allowances for the fact that he has not spoken French for years / You should make allowances for his not speaking French for years / You should make allowances for having spoken French for years / You should make allowances for his having spoken French for years

content.question: 11. This is the first time I've been to the Bahamas.
content.answer: I've never been to the Bahamas before. || I have never been to the Bahamas before. / I've never been to the Bahamas before / I have never been to the Bahamas before

content.question: 12. Only Sheila came up with the solution to the problem.
content.answer: Only Sheila succeeded in coming up with the solution to the problem. || Only Sheila succeeded in finding the solution to the problem. / Only Sheila succeeded in coming up with the solution to the problem / Only Sheila succeeded in finding the solution to the problem

content.question: 13. My boss says I can use his yacht whenever I want to.
content.answer: My boss has put his yacht at my disposal. || My boss says his yacht is at my disposal. / My boss has put his yacht at my disposal / My boss says his yacht is at my disposal

content.question: 14. The news was a shock to us.
content.answer: We were taken aback by the news. || We were taken aback by the news

content.question: 15. James realised that he could never be an architect.
content.answer: James realised that he wasn't cut out to be an architect. || James realised that he was not cut out to be an architect. / James realised that he wasn't cut out to be an architect / James realised that he was not cut out to be an architect

content.question: 16. They chose not to drive because they thought there would be too much snow.
content.answer: They chose not to drive for fear that there would be too much snow. || They chose not to drive for fear that there might be too much snow. / They chose not to drive for fear that there would be too much snow / They chose not to drive for fear that there might be too much snow

content.question: 17. I'm sick of that programme; I've watched it too often.
content.answer: I've gone off that programme. || I have gone off that programme. / I've gone off that programme / I have gone off that programme

content.question: 18. I never have enough time.
content.answer: I am always short of time. || I'm always short of time. / I am always short of time / I'm always short of time

content.question: 19. They tried very hard to finish by midnight.
content.answer: They did their best to finish by midnight. || They did their best to finish by midnight

content.question: 20. The criminal got away through the back window.
content.answer: The criminal made his escape through the back window. || The criminal managed to escape through the back window. / The criminal effected his escape through the back window. / The criminal made his escape through the back window / The criminal managed to escape through the back window / The criminal effected his escape through the back window
